metadata.name: Dependency Resolve
metadata.description: Parses the asset dependency graph for an episode and resolves it into an ordered execution plan. The plan identifies which production tasks must run sequentially (because they depend on each other's outputs) and which can run in parallel (because they are independent). This maximizes throughput while respecting the dependency constraints of the production pipeline.

triggers.trigger: Before episode production begins (after SSD generation).
When the Director needs to plan the execution order for an episode.
When re-planning after a partial failure (re-resolve remaining tasks).

input.name: asset-dependency-graph.yaml
input.description: YAML file defining all assets required for the episode and their dependency relationships. Generated during SSD creation.

outputs.output: Ordered execution plan with parallel groups.
Estimated total duration based on critical path analysis.

graph_schema.description: Expected structure of the asset-dependency-graph.yaml file. Each node in the graph represents an asset or production task. Edges represent "depends on" relationships.

node_schema.field: Human-readable description of what this asset/task produces.
List of node IDs that must complete before this node can execute. Empty or absent means the node has no dependencies and can run in the first parallel group.
Estimated wall-clock time for this task in seconds. Used for critical path analysis and progress estimation.
The tool or skill used to produce this asset (e.g., `dall-e-3`, `runway-gen4`).
The scene this asset belongs to, if applicable.
Whether this task can be retried on failure. Defaults to true.

graph_schema.example_graph:
# asset-dependency-graph.yaml (example)
assets: # Character references — no dependencies, run first - id: char-marcus-ref type: character-ref description: "Generate reference sheet for Marcus" depends_on: [] estimated_duration_seconds: 30 tool: dall-e-3
- id: char-elena-ref type: character-ref description: "Generate reference sheet for Elena" depends_on: [] estimated_duration_seconds: 30 tool: dall-e-3
# First frames — depend on character refs - id: scene-01-frame type: first-frame description: "Generate first frame for scene 01" depends_on: [char-marcus-ref, char-elena-ref] estimated_duration_seconds: 20 tool: dall-e-3 scene_id: "01"
- id: scene-02-frame type: first-frame description: "Generate first frame for scene 02" depends_on: [char-marcus-ref] estimated_duration_seconds: 20 tool: dall-e-3 scene_id: "02"
# Video clips — depend on first frames - id: scene-01-video type: video-clip description: "Generate video clip for scene 01" depends_on: [scene-01-frame] estimated_duration_seconds: 120 tool: runway-gen4 scene_id: "01"
- id: scene-02-video type: video-clip description: "Generate video clip for scene 02" depends_on: [scene-02-frame] estimated_duration_seconds: 120 tool: runway-gen4 scene_id: "02"
# Narration — independent, runs in parallel with video - id: narration-full type: narration description: "Generate full episode narration" depends_on: [] estimated_duration_seconds: 45 tool: elevenlabs
# Music — independent, runs in parallel with everything - id: music-track type: music description: "Generate background music track" depends_on: [] estimated_duration_seconds: 60 tool: suno
# SFX — independent, runs in parallel with everything - id: sfx-pack type: sfx description: "Generate sound effects pack" depends_on: [] estimated_duration_seconds: 30 tool: elevenlabs-sfx
# Final merge — depends on all video clips, narration, music, and sfx - id: episode-merge type: merge description: "Merge all assets into final episode video" depends_on: [scene-01-video, scene-02-video, narration-full, music-track, sfx-pack] estimated_duration_seconds: 60 tool: ffmpeg

resolution_algorithm.description: The dependency resolution algorithm uses topological sorting with level grouping to produce parallel execution groups. It also performs cycle detection to catch invalid dependency configurations.

step.name: Parse Dependency Graph
step.action: Load the asset-dependency-graph.yaml file using the discover-inputs protocol with FULL_LOAD strategy. Parse into an in-memory graph structure with nodes and directed edges.

step.name: Validate Graph Integrity
step.action:
Perform validation checks on the parsed graph:
Every ID in a `depends_on` list must correspond to an existing node in the graph. If a dependency references a non-existent node, trigger `on_error.validation_failure`.
No node may depend on itself. Self-references trigger `on_error.validation_failure`.
The graph must be a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG). Use depth-first search with coloring (white/gray/black) to detect cycles. If a cycle is found, report the cycle path and trigger `on_error.validation_failure`.
Every non-root node should eventually lead to a terminal node (typically a merge node). Warn about orphaned nodes that are not depended upon by anything and are not terminal.

step.name: Compute In-Degree Map
step.action: For each node, count the number of incoming edges (dependencies). Nodes with in-degree 0 have no dependencies and form the first parallel group.

step.name: Topological Sort with Level Grouping
step.action:
Using Kahn's algorithm with level tracking:
Initialize a queue with all nodes that have in-degree 0. These form parallel_group_1.
While the queue is not empty: - Process all nodes in the current level as one parallel group. - For each processed node, decrement the in-degree of all its dependents (nodes that list this node in their depends_on). - Collect all newly zero-in-degree nodes into the next level. - Increment the level counter.
If all nodes have been processed, the sort is complete. If some nodes remain with non-zero in-degree, a cycle exists (this should have been caught in step 2, but serves as a safety net).

step.name: Apply Production Pipeline Rules
step.action:
Apply the COOKIE production pipeline ordering rules on top of the topological sort to ensure correct asset production sequence:
Character reference sheets must be generated before any first-frames that feature those characters. This is enforced by explicit depends_on in the graph, but validated here as a safety check.
All `first-frame` nodes must have at least one `character-ref` node in their transitive dependency set.
First frames must be generated before video clips for the same scene. Video generation uses the first frame as the starting image.
Every `video-clip` node with a scene_id must depend on the corresponding `first-frame` node with the same scene_id.
All video clips must be complete before the merge step.
The `merge` node must depend on all `video-clip` nodes.
Narration generation is independent of the video pipeline. It can run in parallel with first-frame and video-clip generation. It only needs to be complete before the merge step.
`narration` nodes should NOT depend on `first-frame` or `video-clip` nodes. They should only be dependencies of `merge` nodes.
Music and sound effects generation is independent of all other production tasks. They can run in parallel with everything else and only need to be complete before merge.
`music` and `sfx` nodes should have no dependencies (in-degree 0). They should only be dependencies of `merge` nodes.

step.name: Critical Path Analysis
step.action: Identify the critical path (longest path through the DAG by estimated duration) to determine: - The minimum total execution time (assuming unlimited parallelism). - Which tasks are on the critical path (any delay in these delays the entire production). - Which tasks have slack (can be delayed without affecting total production time).

step.name: Generate Execution Plan
step.action: Produce the final execution plan as a structured document containing ordered parallel groups.

execution_plan_format.description: The output of the dependency-resolve protocol is an execution plan that the workflow engine uses to schedule and run production tasks.

schema.field: Unique identifier for this execution plan.
The episode this plan is for.
Total number of tasks in the plan.
Estimated total wall-clock time based on critical path analysis.
Ordered list of task IDs on the critical path.

Ordered array of parallel groups. Each group contains tasks that can execute simultaneously. Groups must execute in order (group N+1 only starts when all tasks in group N are complete).
Zero-based index of this group in the execution order.
List of tasks in this group that can run in parallel.
Task node ID from the dependency graph. Asset type. Tool to use. Estimated duration. Whether this task is on the critical path. Available slack time before this task delays the plan.
Duration of the longest task in this group (determines when the group completes, since tasks run in parallel).

execution_plan_format.example_plan:
plan_id: "dep-resolve-ep01-20260301T140000-b2c4" episode_id: "ep01" total_tasks: 9 estimated_duration_seconds: 230 critical_path: - char-marcus-ref - scene-01-frame - scene-01-video - episode-merge
parallel_groups: - group_index: 0 group_estimated_duration_seconds: 60 tasks: - id: char-marcus-ref type: character-ref tool: dall-e-3 estimated_duration_seconds: 30 on_critical_path: true slack_seconds: 0 - id: char-elena-ref type: character-ref tool: dall-e-3 estimated_duration_seconds: 30 on_critical_path: false slack_seconds: 30 - id: narration-full type: narration tool: elevenlabs estimated_duration_seconds: 45 on_critical_path: false slack_seconds: 125 - id: music-track type: music tool: suno estimated_duration_seconds: 60 on_critical_path: false slack_seconds: 110 - id: sfx-pack type: sfx tool: elevenlabs-sfx estimated_duration_seconds: 30 on_critical_path: false slack_seconds: 140
- group_index: 1 group_estimated_duration_seconds: 20 tasks: - id: scene-01-frame type: first-frame tool: dall-e-3 estimated_duration_seconds: 20 on_critical_path: true slack_seconds: 0 - id: scene-02-frame type: first-frame tool: dall-e-3 estimated_duration_seconds: 20 on_critical_path: false slack_seconds: 0
- group_index: 2 group_estimated_duration_seconds: 120 tasks: - id: scene-01-video type: video-clip tool: runway-gen4 estimated_duration_seconds: 120 on_critical_path: true slack_seconds: 0 - id: scene-02-video type: video-clip tool: runway-gen4 estimated_duration_seconds: 120 on_critical_path: false slack_seconds: 0
- group_index: 3 group_estimated_duration_seconds: 60 tasks: - id: episode-merge type: merge tool: ffmpeg estimated_duration_seconds: 60 on_critical_path: true slack_seconds: 0

replanning.description: When a workflow partially fails (some tasks succeed, others fail), the dependency-resolve protocol can re-plan the remaining work. This avoids re-executing tasks that already succeeded.

procedure.step: Receive the list of completed task IDs from the handoff document of the failed workflow run.
Remove completed tasks from the dependency graph.
For remaining tasks, update their depends_on lists to remove references to completed tasks (since those dependencies are already satisfied).
Re-run the resolution algorithm on the reduced graph.
Produce a new execution plan covering only the remaining tasks. Include a `replanning_context` field that references the original plan ID and lists which tasks were carried over.

on_error.description: Triggered when the dependency graph fails validation (cycles, dangling references, missing required dependencies).
on_error.action: Halt the workflow. The dependency graph must be fixed before production can proceed. Provide a detailed error report: - The specific validation check that failed. - The nodes involved in the failure. - For cycles: the full cycle path. - For dangling references: the missing node ID and which node references it. - Suggestions for fixing the graph.

on_error.description: Triggered when the asset-dependency-graph.yaml file is missing.
on_error.action: Delegate to error-recovery.xml missing_file handler. The graph file is critical; the workflow cannot proceed without it. Suggest running the SSD generation workflow first.
